recurringPattern: Grid of metric cards each containing a small trend icon (up green arrow / down red arrow), a large number, a short label, and a miniature sparkline chart. Cards have identical sizing with rounded corners and subtle shadows.
designHarm: The uniform card format hides metric priority — every number looks equally important. The green-up/red-down color coding creates emotional reactions before users read the metric context. Sparkline charts in every card add visual noise when most users only need the current value and a comparison. The pattern encourages dashboard designers to show 12+ metrics when only 3-4 drive decisions.
legitimateExceptions: Executive dashboards where many metrics need at-a-glance monitoring
possibleFixtures: Focused analytics view with one primary metric and supporting data
Text-only metric display without sparklines or trend arrows
originalObservations: AI-generated analytics cards always have identical dimensions regardless of metric value length or importance.
The trend arrows and percentage changes appear on every card even when the change is negligible.
Sparkline charts use the brand's primary color for the line with a subtle gradient fill underneath.
